t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: …不久前，浮黎的目光掠过了翁法罗斯。加上黑塔女士此行获知的情报——
t_en: Not long ago, Fuli's gaze swept across Amphoreus. Combined with the intel Madam Herta gathered on this journey—

t_ch: 翁法罗斯或许将会引燃<color=#dbc291ff>一位绝灭大君的怒火</color>。
t_en: Amphoreus might soon trigger <color=#dbc291ff>the rage of a Lord Ravager</color>.

t_ch: 绝灭大君…此前我们在仙舟曾与绝灭大君幻胧对峙，在那之后也从仙舟处了解了一些相关信息。
t_en: Lord Ravager Phantylia... We confronted her on the Xianzhou, and later gained some relevant information from her.

t_ch: 不知翁法罗斯背后暗中酝酿的毁灭，是来自早已闻名寰宇的焚风、星啸、铁墓，还是像幻胧这样尚不为世人所知的大君。
t_en: I wonder if the destruction secretly brewing behind Amphoreus comes from renowned Overlords like Zephyro, Celenova, and Irontomb, or from one still unknown to the world, like Phantylia.

t_ch: 无论是哪位…一旦发作，必然导致生灵涂炭。
t_en: Whichever one it is... when it erupts, countless lives will surely be lost.

t_ch: 呵，从这个角度看，翁法罗斯或许是个安全匣呢。毕竟，三股令使级别甚至更高的命途之力，一旦擦枪走火，可比查德威克那家伙的炸药带劲多了。
t_en: Hmph, when you look at it that way, Amphoreus might be a safety box. After all, three forces at the Emanator level or even higher — if they were to come to blows, it would be far more explosive than Chadwick's bombs.

t_ch: 若真如此…身在翁法罗斯内部的{NICKNAME}和丹恒，岂不是相当危险？
t_en: If that's true... wouldn't {NICKNAME} and Dan Heng be in extreme danger in Amphoreus?

t_ch: 无论如何…我们必须联系上他们。
t_en: In any case... we must reach them somehow.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡。靠近时，能依稀听到一些零散的字词，例如…「来古士」。
t_en: Bubbles drift through the air. When you get close, you can dimly make out disconnected words, including... "Lygus."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 听黑塔女士的叙述，那位叫来古士的智械，同样沐浴过博识尊的瞥视。有这样一位人物把守翁法罗斯，恐怕我们的计划会举步维艰。
t_en: Based on Madam Herta's account, that Intellitron named Lygus has also been bathed in Nous's gaze. Having such an entity guarding Amphoreus will likely make our plans extremely difficult to execute.

t_ch: 放心，我早有准备。要用魔法打败魔法，用科学打败科学……
t_en: Fear not, I've come prepared. We'll fight magic with magic, and science with science...

t_ch: 用机器脑袋打败机器脑袋。
t_en: Beating machine brain with machine brain.

t_ch: 承蒙黑塔女士信任。但在我们展开讨论之前，我仍有一个问题想要请教。
t_en: I appreciate Madam Herta's confidence in me. But before we delve into this discussion, there's something I must ask.

t_ch: 黑塔女士，<color=#dbc291ff>你果真进入了翁法罗斯的世界吗？</color>
t_en: Madam Herta, <color=#dbc291ff>did you actually enter the world of Amphoreus?</color>

t_ch: 螺丝咕姆先生的意思是，黑塔女士进入的翁法罗斯，有可能是那位来古士构建的认知障碍…就像同谐之力那样？
t_en: Mr. Screwllum means that the Amphoreus you entered, Madam Herta, might have been a cognitive barrier constructed by Lygus... similar to the power of Harmony?

t_ch: 想要影响我的认知还不被我察觉…哪怕是同谐的令使也未必做得到。
t_en: Manipulating my cognition without my awareness... that might be beyond even an Emanator of Harmony.

t_ch: 螺丝，你想说，我所到的世界…只是一道防火墙？
t_en: Screwy, are you saying the world I entered... was just a firewall?

t_ch: 正是。而且，那恐怕并不是一道简单的防火墙。
t_en: Precisely. And I fear it wasn't a simple firewall at that.

t_ch: 翁法罗斯以空间作为第一道屏障，对黑塔女士来说，跨越这道屏障易如反掌。
t_en: Amphoreus uses space as its first barrier. For Madam Herta, crossing this barrier is child's play.

t_ch: 可那第二道屏障…名为时间。
t_en: However, the second barrier... is time itself.

t_ch: 原来如此。黑塔女士提到，她在翁法罗斯的世界里看到过两位无名客的残像，只是那看上去既像残留的忆质，又像数据的投影。
t_en: That explains it. Madam Herta mentioned observing traces of the two Nameless in the world of Amphoreus, which appeared to be both residual memoria and data materialization.

t_ch: 这么看来，在不同的时间，{NICKNAME}和丹恒的步履曾经与黑塔女士重叠。
t_en: Which means that at various moments, {NICKNAME} and Dan Heng's paths have overlapped with Madam Herta's.

t_ch: 往好处想，这至少说明他们二人确实安全降落，并且展开了较为成功的开拓。
t_en: On the bright side, this at least confirms they landed safely and made some successful progress in their Trailblaze.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡。靠近时，能依稀听到一些零散的字词，例如…「帝皇权杖」。
t_en: Bubbles drift through the air. When you get close, you can dimly make out disconnected words, including... "Emperor's Scepter."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 以时空作为屏障的世界，在我所经历的漫长岁月中，确实曾经见到过……
t_en: In my long existence, I have certainly come across worlds that use space-time as barriers...

t_ch: 各位可曾听说过……<color=#dbc291ff>帝皇权杖</color>？
t_en: Are any of you familiar with... the <color=#dbc291ff>Emperor's Scepter</color>?

t_ch: 鲁珀特二世所拥的诸多权杖中，以反生命程式驱动、专司于模拟万千文明毁灭路径的巨型系统……
t_en: A colossal system among Emperor Rubert II's collection of scepters, operated by antilife equations and created to simulate destruction scenarios for innumerable civilizations...

t_ch: 即使在学派战争中，其余权杖的算力被榨干时，也无人敢动用那种不受控的智识杀器——它们要么被永久封存，要么被远弃星渊。
t_en: Even amid the Scholars' Strife, when other scepters were depleted of computing power, none dared employ such ungovernable Erudition weapons — they were either sealed forever or cast into distant stellar abysses.

t_ch: 螺丝，你在哪儿见过这等不祥之物？
t_en: Screwy, where did you come across such a sinister artifact?

t_ch: 于茫茫边星寻觅无机生命的起源时，我曾见过一台漏网之鱼。
t_en: While exploring the origins of inorganic life across distant border stars, I found one that had escaped detection.

t_ch: 即便其主早已在「自我加冕」中身陨，那台权杖也仍未停止运作。可怖的算力潮汐于其中涌动，却从未向外界漾出一丝波澜。
t_en: Even though its operator had perished in "Self-Coronation," that scepter continued to function. Terrifying computational tides surged within it, yet not a single ripple escaped to the outside world.

t_ch: 为了使演算不受外界干扰，帝皇权杖将己身隐没于凹陷空间，并主动扭曲了内部的时间流速。
t_en: To protect its extrapolations from external disruption, the Emperor's Scepter secluded itself in a concave space and deliberately warped its internal time flow.

t_ch: 权杖内部的世界，则在千百年的演算中逐渐趋于疯狂。我尝试向内窥探，却险些被那疯溃的数据实体扰乱了逻辑原件。
t_en: The world inside the scepter gradually descended into madness after centuries of extrapolation. When I attempted to peer inside, I nearly had my logical components disrupted by those insane data entities.

t_ch: 判断：倘若那台帝皇权杖运算失控，可能会再度引发一场无机生命主导的灾难。于是我花费两个琥珀纪的时间，从外部将其瘫痪。
t_en: Conclusion: Should that Emperor's Scepter's extrapolations run amok, it might instigate another calamity driven by inorganic life. Consequently, I devoted two Amber Eras to disable it from without.

t_ch: 推论：翁法罗斯的正身也许是一台亡佚的帝皇权杖，或其他类似的智识造物；而那位来古士便是权杖的操作员…或是隐居世外的又一位鲁珀特。
t_en: Deduction: Amphoreus's actual identity might be a lost Emperor's Scepter or similar Erudition construct, while Lygus may be its operator... or perhaps another Rubert hidden from the world.

t_ch: 很难想象，运算了上百个琥珀纪的权杖，其内部会是多么疯狂的光景。
t_en: It's hard to imagine the madness within a scepter that has been extrapolating for over a hundred Amber Eras.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡。靠近时，能依稀听到一些零散的字词，例如…「识刻锚」。
t_en: Bubbles drift through the air. When you get close, you can dimly make out disconnected words, including... "Chronocognitive Anchor."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 为了探得权杖内部的讯息，我曾花费半个琥珀纪的时间，制作出一枚<color=#dbc291ff>识刻锚</color>。
t_en: To explore information inside the scepter, I once spent half an Amber Era creating a <color=#dbc291ff>Chronocognitive Anchor</color>.

t_ch: 这便是我为诸位带来的礼物。彼时，我将它嵌入帝皇权杖后，便在不摧毁其外壳的前提下，获得了一份锚定的时间坐标。
t_en: This is the gift I bring to you all. After implanting it into the Emperor's Scepter then, I secured a set of anchored temporal coordinates without compromising its shell.

t_ch: 即使世界内时间流遭打乱，基本的因果也不可悖反。在运算的最小单位中，其时间流向依然是有矩可循的。
t_en: Even when a world's temporal flow is disordered, basic causality remains inviolable. In the smallest computational increments, time's flow still follows discernible patterns.

t_ch: 逻辑：若来古士为翁法罗斯打造的防火墙与帝皇权杖的自卫机制相似，那么识刻锚也应当对其起效。
t_en: Logic: If the firewall Lygus created for Amphoreus is similar to the Emperor's Scepter's self-defense mechanism, then the Chronocognitive Anchor should be effective against it as well.

t_ch: …螺丝，我上一次这么佩服你，还是一起建造模拟宇宙的时候。
t_en: Oh Screwy, the last time I was impressed was when we built the Simulated Universe together.

t_ch: 黑塔女士谬赞了，不过是一个食而无味、弃之可惜的小发明。
t_en: You flatter me, Madam Herta. It's just a minor invention — not satisfying to use but serves its purpose.

t_ch: 虽然还有些许疑惑之处，但螺丝咕姆先生带来的这份礼物，想必有助于我们联络上{NICKNAME}和丹恒。
t_en: While I still have some questions, Mr. Screwllum's gift will surely help us reach {NICKNAME} and Dan Heng.

t_ch: 前提：我们拥有那两位勇敢无名客的时间坐标，且识刻锚确实能对翁法罗斯起效。
t_en: Premises: We have the temporal coordinates of those two brave Nameless, and the Chronocognitive Anchor actually works on Amphoreus.

t_ch: 前者好办。我在防火墙见到过他俩的残像，用作参照系正合适。至于后者……
t_en: The first is easy. I've seen their residual memoria in the firewall, which work perfectly as reference points. As for the latter...

t_ch: ——不试试怎么知道呢？
t_en: Well, there's only one way to find out.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你看，这里也和记忆中的哀丽秘榭一模一样。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, look! This place looks exactly like how I remember Aedes Elysiae.

t_ch: 再等一阵风经过，麦穗被吹拂的时候，还要更好看呢。要是有办法记录下来就好啦。
t_en: Once the wind blows through again and the wheat starts swaying, it'll be even more beautiful. If only we could capture it somehow.

t_ch: 留影石机？对喔，可以把风景分毫不差地保存下来，真是伟大的造物。
t_en: A photostone? Oh, right! It can save the scene perfectly, what a clever invention!

t_ch: 可惜，储存卡满了。
t_en: Too bad the memory card's full.

t_ch: 也就是说，它暂时装不下更多回忆了，是吗？
t_en: So... no more room for memories, huh?

t_ch: 没关系哦，试着拍拍看吧。在哀丽秘榭，偶尔也会有奇迹发生。
t_en: It's okay. Give it a little tap. Sometimes small miracles happen in Aedes Elysiae.

t_ch: 光线正好呢，可以让我试试吗？
t_en: The lighting's just right. Mind if I give it a try?

t_ch: （把相机递给昔涟）
t_en: *hands the camera to Cyrene*

t_ch: 谢谢~我就知道，{NICKNAME}最好啦。
t_en: Thanks~ I knew I could count on you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 哎呀，怎么露出惊讶的神情了？值得被铭记的瞬间里，怎么可能没有你呢？
t_en: Huh? What's with that surprised look? How could a moment worth remembering not include you?

t_ch: 不如说，{NICKNAME}才是这一幕的主角呢。
t_en: Honestly, {NICKNAME}, you're the star of the whole scene.

t_ch: 好啦好啦。笑一笑，或者做点其他喜欢的表情，要开始咯？
t_en: Alright, alright. Give me a smile, or make whatever face you like. Ready?

t_ch: 五…四…三…
t_en: Five... four... three...
... One!

t_ch: 嘻，没想到吧？比起摆好姿势，抓拍到的意外才更令人惊喜呢。{NICKNAME}刚才的表情很棒哦。
t_en: Hehe, didn't see that coming, did you? Candid shots are way more fun than posed ones. That expression was perfect, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 只是很可惜，这台留影石机确实装不下更多回忆啦……
t_en: It's just a shame the photostone really can't save any more memories...

t_ch: 奇迹没有发生啊。
t_en: No miracle this time.

t_ch: 当然，储存卡满了。
t_en: Yeah, the card's full, after all.

t_ch: 可是没关系。比起一张照片，「留影」的心情才更值得纪念。「给{NICKNAME}拍了照片」…就这么记住今天的回忆，也足够温馨啦。
t_en: But hey, it's not about the photo, it's the feeling that matters. Just thinking "I took a photo of {NICKNAME}" is enough to make today special.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}不也这么认为吗？所以才会随身带着这台留影石机，有它在，就相当于同伴一直陪伴在你身边。
t_en: Don't you think so too, {NICKNAME}? Maybe that's why you always carry this photostone around. With it, it's like your friends are always right there with you.

t_ch: 再说啦，也可以当作是在销毁证据？没有照片留下，我就可以把自己美化得更瘦一些。
t_en: Plus, you could think of it as... destroying the evidence. No photo means I can pretend I looked a little slimmer.

t_ch: 你呢，{NICKNAME}？以后回想起来，会怎么想象今天的我？
t_en: What about you, {NICKNAME}? When you think back on today, how will you picture me?

t_ch: 就是现在的样子。
t_en: Just like you are now.

t_ch: 会是更好的模样。
t_en: Even more beautiful.

t_ch: 深不可测的神秘人。
t_en: Mysterious and unreadable.

t_ch: 会更胖一些。
t_en: A bit chubbier.

t_ch: 嘻，本来还想说，唯独这个不可以呢。不过……
t_en: Hehe, I was gonna say "everything but that one"! But...

t_ch: 人家倒是不担心啦。以后我们还会遇见许多人或事，并因此回忆起今天的一些心绪……
t_en: I'm not too worried. We'll meet all kinds of people and experience all sorts of things in the future, and some of those moments will bring us right back to how we felt today...

t_ch: 到那时，我们也有机会，把记忆中的对方装点得更美丽些，对吧？
t_en: When that happens, we'll get to paint even lovelier versions of each other in our memories, won't we?

t_ch: 呼~感觉有点困了…是时候闭上眼睛，去梦里相见啦……
t_en: *yawn*~ I'm feeling kinda sleepy... Time for some shuteye. See you in dreamland...

t_ch: 怎么在这里停下啦？难道说，{NICKNAME}对钓鱼感兴趣？
t_en: Why'd you stop here? Wait... don't tell me you're into fishing, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 有些兴趣。
t_en: Kinda.

t_ch: 真的能钓上鱼？
t_en: Think we'll actually catch some fish?

t_ch: 反正也无事可做。
t_en: Not like we've got anything better to do.

t_ch: 悄悄告诉你，哀丽秘榭的鱼儿都会读心魔法，除非心甘情愿，不然绝对不会上钩。
t_en: Wanna know a secret? The fish in Aedes Elysiae can read minds. Unless they're totally willing, they won't bite no matter what.

t_ch: 怎么样，要试试看吗？就当是一次魅力检定？
t_en: So? Wanna give it a try? Consider it a charm check.

t_ch: 下竿之后，总是免不了漫长的等待呀。
t_en: Once the line's cast, there's nothing to do but wait...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}听过这样的说法吗？两个人独处时，难能可贵的不是说不完的话题，而是沉默下来也不会尴尬的默契。
t_en: Has anyone ever told you this, {NICKNAME}? When two people are alone, what's truly special isn't having endless things to talk about, it's being able to sit in silence without it feeling awkward.

t_ch: 就像现在，{NICKNAME}心里在想什么呢？介意与我分享吗？
t_en: Kind of like now. What's on your mind, {NICKNAME}? Want to share it with me?

t_ch: 在想哀丽秘榭的事。
t_en: I'm thinking about Aedes Elysiae.

t_ch: 对这里感到好奇吗？与世隔绝的小村庄，我的故乡，下一趟「开拓」之旅的起点……
t_en: Curious about it? A little village cut off from the world... My hometown... and the starting point of our next Trailblazing expedition.

t_ch: 暂时只有这些啦。更多的秘密，就等我们一起揭开吧。
t_en: That's all for now. As for the rest of the secrets, we'll uncover them together.

t_ch: 在沉思你为什么认识我。
t_en: I'm thinking about how you even know me.

t_ch: 也许答案很简单，只需小小换位思考一下：{NICKNAME}为什么认识我呢？
t_en: Maybe it's a simple answer. Try flipping the question: Why does {NICKNAME} know me?

t_ch: 因为我们一同经历过很多事。在朦胧的记忆里，人家可是作为要好的伙伴，在{NICKNAME}身边待了很久呢。
t_en: Because we've been through so much together. Even in those blurry memories, I was always by {NICKNAME}'s side as a close friend.

t_ch: 没什么别的了。
t_en: That's really all there is to it.

t_ch: 嗯，像这样把心里话全部说出来，就不会觉得有负担啦。
t_en: Mm. Letting it all out makes your heart feel a little lighter, doesn't it?

t_ch: 不过，要是人类也能学会读心魔法就好了，只要注视彼此就能传递心意……
t_en: Still, wouldn't it be nice if people could use mind-reading magic? Just one look, and you'd know exactly how someone feels...

t_ch: 啊，水面有动静了！快快，把鱼竿提起来，拜托啦！
t_en: Look, ripples! C'mon, reel it in! Please!

t_ch: 你钓上了…一个泳圈？
t_en: You caught... a swim ring?

t_ch: 你比了比泳圈的大小和你的腰围…完全不合身。该不会是哪只小海豹落在这儿的吧？
t_en: You compare the size of that swim ring to your waist... definitely not a match. Did some baby seal leave this behind?

t_ch: 瞧啊，{NICKNAME}，好丰盛的果盆。看到生活中的小幸福，总是让人心情喜悦。
t_en: Look, {NICKNAME}, a whole plate of fresh fruit. Little joys like this always make me smile.

t_ch: 它总能提醒我，这个世界依然热热闹闹。有许多人在努力地生活。
t_en: They remind me that the world's still bustling, still full of people doing their best to live.

t_ch: 能理解这种感受。
t_en: I know what you mean.

t_ch: 有些多愁善感吧？
t_en: Feeling a little sentimental, huh?

t_ch: 那，不妨来预演一下吧？假如有一天，{NICKNAME}身边的人突然消失了……
t_en: Then how about a little warm-up? Imagine someone close to {NICKNAME} suddenly disappears...

t_ch: 就像现在这样。你会怎么做呢？
t_en: Just like now. What would you do?

t_ch: 昔涟，去哪儿了？
t_en: Cyrene, where did you go?

t_ch: 嘻，开个玩笑，只是气氛正好，想和你玩捉迷藏。提示：<color=#dbc291ff>人家就在你身边哦。</color>
t_en: Hehe, just kidding. The mood felt right, so I thought I'd play a little Hide-and-Seek. Here's a hint: <color=#dbc291ff>I'm right next to you.</color>

t_ch: （我的身边…？）
t_en: (Right next to me...?)

t_ch: 你环顾四周，祝祭庭院、打谷场、海边…都没有昔涟的身影。安静的村落中，只有轻风作响。
t_en: You glance around — the Sacrament Courtyard, the threshing grounds, the beach... but there's no sign of Cyrene. Just the soft rustle of wind through the quiet village.

t_ch: 直到你收回视线，大树、秋千、池塘，还有初次见面时她送你的稻草人……
t_en: Until your eyes returned to the tree, the swing, the pond... and the scarecrow she gave you when you first met.

t_ch: 稻草人？
t_en: The scarecrow?

t_ch: 这不就是「我」？
t_en: That's me.

t_ch: 果然被发现啦。你知道吗？捉迷藏是检验两个人心有灵犀的最佳方式哦。
t_en: Knew you'd find me. Hide-and-Seek's the best way to see if two people are truly connected.

t_ch: 就像「记忆」一样。在时间的长河里，它可能会消失，但不会离去。当内心的小角落被唤醒，回忆一定会以更美的形式再度浮现。
t_en: It's like memories. They might fade with time, but they never really go away. When the heart is stirred, those moments return, more vivid and more beautiful than before.

t_ch: 原来如此。
t_en: I see.

t_ch: 有这种说法吗？
t_en: Is that a real saying?

t_ch: 有，人家说的。是不是很有道理？
t_en: It is. I just said it. Makes sense, doesn't it?

t_ch: 也许当我们再度踏上旅途，和{NICKNAME}玩捉迷藏的就不是我，而是那触不可及的命运了。不过我相信，无论多少次，{NICKNAME}一定都能……
t_en: Maybe next time we head out on a journey, it won't be me playing Hide-and-Seek with {NICKNAME}, it'll be fate, just out of reach. But I believe, no matter how many times, {NICKNAME}'ll always...

t_ch: 哎呀，太沉重的话题可不适合哀丽秘榭。还是聊回捉迷藏吧，作为交换，接下来轮到我来找你咯？
t_en: Ah, but that's too heavy a thought for a place like Aedes Elysiae. Let's go back to Hide-and-Seek. Now it's my turn to find you, okay?

t_ch: 我会躲到你找不到的地方。
t_en: I'll hide somewhere you'll never find me.

t_ch: 嘻，那我就一直一直一直找下去。
t_en: Hehe, then I'll just keep looking... and looking... and looking.

t_ch: 别让我等太久。
t_en: Don't keep me waiting too long.

t_ch: 嗯，这也是人家想说的话呢。
t_en: Mm, that's exactly what I was going to say.

t_ch: 嗯…哪里去了呢？偷偷躲在哪个小角落里，等着我去找呢？
t_en: Hmm... Now where did it go? Hiding in some corner, waiting for me to come find it?

t_ch: 迷路迷境的入口，无论看多少次都觉得很梦幻……
t_en: No matter how many times I see the entrance to the Membrance Maze, it still feels like a dream...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}会感同身受吗？光是站在这里，就有种将要踏上奇幻冒险的预感。
t_en: Do you feel it too, {NICKNAME}? Just standing here makes it feel like a magical adventure's about to begin.

t_ch: 事实的确如此。
t_en: Indeed.

t_ch: 小妖精们很害羞，据说只有天真无邪的孩子才能进入迷境。
t_en: Fairies are very shy. Word has it that only pure and innocent children can enter the maze.

t_ch: 但我相信，{NICKNAME}一定也在受欢迎的名单中，要试试看吗？
t_en: But I trust that {NICKNAME} will be welcome. How about giving it a try?

t_ch: 村民不知道它的存在吗？
t_en: Don't the villagers know of its existence?

t_ch: 是呀。小妖精们很害羞，只有天真无邪的孩子才能进入迷境。
t_en: Yes, fairies are very shy. Only pure and innocent children can enter the maze.

t_ch: 但我相信，{NICKNAME}一定也在受欢迎的名单中，要试试看吗？
t_en: But I trust that {NICKNAME} will be welcome. How about giving it a try?

t_ch: 金色的蝴蝶，可以捉吗…
t_en: A golden butterfly... Can I catch it?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，思绪飘去哪儿了呀？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, why are you in a daze?

t_ch: …算啦，这也是你的特质。据说只有天真无邪的孩子才能进入迷境。
t_en: ...Never mind, that's just how you are. Word has it that only pure and innocent children can enter the maze.

t_ch: 我相信，{NICKNAME}一定也符合受欢迎的标准，要试试看吗？
t_en: I trust that {NICKNAME} has the traits of those who can enter. How about giving it a try?

t_ch: （进入树洞）
t_en: (Enters the tree hollow)

t_ch: 嗯哼，果然很顺利呢。就这样小心前进……
t_en: Going smoothly, just as I expected. Be careful on your way in...

t_ch: <size=28>迷——！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Memememe!</size>

t_ch: 什、什么声音？{NICKNAME}，没受伤吧？
t_en: Wh—What's that sound? {NICKNAME}, are you okay?

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}没受伤，我受伤迷…快起来…压到我了迷…！
t_en: {F#She's}{M#He's} fine, but I'm not mem... Get up... You're crushing me mem...

t_ch: 人类…太重了迷！
t_en: Humans... are so heavy mem!

t_ch: 哎呀…第一次进入迷境时，「昔涟」似乎也遇见过同样的事呢。
t_en: Oh my... "Cyrene" also encountered something similar when she first entered the maze.

t_ch: 小妖精，真对不起，你是来欢迎新访客的吧？作为道歉，我们会用欧洛尼斯的小魔法为你治疗。
t_en: I'm sorry, little fairy. Are you here to welcome the new visitors? As an apology, we'll heal you with Oronyx's magic.

t_ch: 没关系，迷的身子很结实，只想要小小的补偿！
t_en: It's okay. Mem's very sturdy, but I'd just like a small compensation!

t_ch: 迷是来听故事的，给迷讲个外面的故事就好！要优美动听的！
t_en: Mem's here to listen to stories. Tell mem a story about the outside world and that'll do! Something wonderful and moving!

t_ch: 想听故事吗？我有好多好多，可以一个个讲给你听。条件只有一个——要仔细听，好吗？
t_en: A story? I have so many I can tell you, but you'll have to listen to each and every one carefully. Alright?

t_ch: 于是，昔涟讲起《如我所书》的故事……
t_en: Cyrene begins narrating the stories in "As I've Written"...

t_ch: 呼…小妖精满意地离开了，它平安无事就好。
t_en: Phew... The fairy is finally satisfied and willing to leave. I'm glad it wasn't hurt.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}呢，习惯了讲述故事，这次作为听众感觉如何？
t_en: What about you, {NICKNAME}? How does it feel to be an audience after being a narrator for so long?

t_ch: 感觉你对这里还挺熟悉。
t_en: You seem familiar with this place.

t_ch: 真的谈不上熟悉哦？不如说…有些缺乏实感呢。
t_en: Not exactly... Rather... it feels disconnected from reality.

t_ch: 以前的记忆，能想起更多吗？
t_en: Can you recall more of those lost memories?

t_ch: 似乎不行。不如说，有些缺乏实感呢。
t_en: This won't do. Somehow, it feels disconnected from reality.

t_ch: 也许过去的「昔涟」在迷境经历过什么，但我的记忆却很模糊。和{NICKNAME}一样，甚至有种初次拜访的感觉……
t_en: Perhaps "Cyrene" experienced something in the maze before, but my memory is a blur. Just like {NICKNAME}, it feels like my first time visiting this place...

t_ch: 不过别担心，至少「迷迷」时期的记忆有好好保存下来哦，比如那段逐火之旅的细节、《如我所书》、还有……
t_en: But don't worry. My memories from being Mem have been retained. I can remember the Flame-Chase Journey, "As I've Written," and...

t_ch: 还有……
t_en: And...

t_ch: …还好，人家难以启齿的记忆，只有你知道呢。不提这个了，跟人家在迷境里多探索一会儿吧？
t_en: ...It's okay. Only you know the memories I can't bring myself to speak of. Let's put that aside. Why don't you stay with me a little longer in this maze and explore together?

t_ch: 昔涟拉着你在迷境中转了许久…直到你们都累了，她打着哈欠向你道别。
t_en: Cyrene dragged you around the Maze for a good while... Eventually, both of you were worn out. She yawned and said goodbye.

t_ch: （所以，到底「还有」什么？等昔涟休息好了再去问她吧……）
t_en: (So what else did she want to say after "and"? Guess you'll have to ask once she's rested...)

t_ch: 在微风和煦的日子里，躺在阳台上，看着风车慢悠悠地转动，别提多惬意啦。
t_en: It's such a pleasure to lie on the balcony and watch the windmills languidly turn on a sunny and breezy day.

t_ch: 告诉你一个秘密，这些在风中转悠的大家伙其实不是摆设哦。
t_en: Let me tell you a secret. These big contraptions that turn amidst the winds aren't just decorations.

t_ch: 在它们的肚子里面放着磨盘，村民会借助风车来磨碎谷物，用面粉做出香喷喷的面包。
t_en: They have mills in them. The villagers use windmills to grind grain and bake tasty bread from flour.

t_ch: 现在，不如就和我一起躺下来，放松地观赏风车们吧…睡着了也没关系，人家会端来面包，用热腾腾的香气叫醒你的。
t_en: Why don't we lie down and enjoy the windmills together? It's okay to fall asleep. Someone will bring you bread and the warm fragrance will wake you up.

t_ch: 风车转呀转，转得好慢好慢，仿佛连时间也停下来了呢……
t_en: Round and round the windmill spins, so slow, oh so slow, so slow that it's like even time itself has stopped...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，就在这边稍微坐会儿，好不好？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, can we sit here for a short while?

t_ch: 对了，不如…把鞋子脱掉，一起感受下水温吧。{NICKNAME}有这样试过吗？
t_en: Oh... why don't you take off your shoes and dip your feet into the water? {NICKNAME}, have you tried this before?

t_ch: 呼~好凉好凉。
t_en: Phew~ It's refreshing.

t_ch: 不觉得这样很浪漫吗？海面上的波纹就像有魔法，如果盯着看，时间不知不觉就会过去很久。
t_en: Don't you find this romantic? The ripples on the sea surface are like magic. If you stare at them, time flies by before you even realize.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你说…海的另一头会是什么呢？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what do you think... lies at the other end of the sea?

t_ch: 海的那边…是敌人！
t_en: On the other side... there are enemies!

t_ch: 呀…那等他们打过来的时候，{NICKNAME}可要保护好大家哦？
t_en: Oh... Then when they attack, you'll have to protect everyone.

t_ch: 是…鳞渊境。
t_en: It's... Scalegorge Waterscape.

t_ch: 鳞渊境？很美妙的名字呢，真叫人浮想联翩。要是能有艘船在码头靠岸，载着我们去那里冒险就好啦……
t_en: Scalegorge Waterscape? That's a beautiful name that fuels the imagination. If only we had a ship to sail over to the docks and take us on an adventure...

t_ch: 不如去亲眼看看。
t_en: Why don't you find out for yourself?

t_ch: 现在可不敢啦，人家本来就不会游泳。现在，甚至连飞都飞不起来了。
t_en: I'm no longer brave enough for that now, and I can't swim. In fact, I can't even fly anymore.

t_ch: 我不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 嘻，我也经常这么想。只要装作不知道，就可以把它想象得更美好些。
t_en: Heh, that's what I think too. As long as I feign ignorance, I can always fantasize about something better.

t_ch: 好像在许多故事里，大海扮演的角色都很可怕呢。不是世界的尽头，就是灾难的象征。
t_en: It seems that in many stories, the sea plays a terrifying role, signifying the end of the world or symbolizing disasters.

t_ch: 但那些最勇敢的水手，在征服大海后，往往都会抵达美丽的仙境，见证壮阔的星空。
t_en: But the bravest sailors who conquer the sea always reach a beautiful paradise under a magnificent starry sky.

t_ch: 这么说来，「星空」也经常被比作海洋呢。也许所有引人遐想的事物，本质都是相似的？
t_en: Oh, "starry sky" is often used to allude to the sea too. Perhaps the things that spark the imagination are similar in essence.

t_ch: 所以来自天外的{NICKNAME}，是不是也能算作一种「勇敢的水手」呢？
t_en: As for you who come from beyond the sky, are you also a kind of "brave sailor"?

t_ch: 好舒服的风呀，真希望生活每天都能像海面一样平静。
t_en: Wow, the breeze is so pleasant! I wish every day was as peaceful as the surface of the sea.

t_ch: 打谷场…要是从天上看，会不会像是一座被麦浪簇拥的小岛？
t_en: Threshing grounds... If we looked down from the sky, would this look like an island surrounded by ripples of wheat?

t_ch: 这么说来，哀丽秘榭到处都很安静呢。待在这种让人心静的地方，无论什么烦恼，都能一下子忘掉……
t_en: Come to think of it, Aedes Elysiae is such a quiet place. Residing in such a serene place instantly dispels all your troubles.

t_ch: 你有很多烦恼吗？
t_en: Do you have a lot of troubles?

t_ch: 当然啦。怀揣烦恼的少女，听起来就很有魅力，对吧？
t_en: Certainly. Doesn't a troubled girl sound rather charming?

t_ch: 好像闻得到麦香。
t_en: I smell wheat.

t_ch: 咦，我怎么闻不到……
t_en: Huh? Why don't I smell it...

t_ch: 呀，意外收获，空气里充满了别人家的炊香呢。
t_en: Oh, an unexpected discovery. The smell of cooking is in the air.

t_ch: 好啦，把可爱的心思收一收，我们一起在附近走走吧。
t_en: Alright, tuck your lovely thoughts aside. Let's take a walk around.

t_ch: 说起来，这里也有一个小稻草人。但因为太平易近人了，没什么威慑力。
t_en: Speaking of which, there's a small scarecrow here. But it looks too friendly to prove useful, lacks the fear factor.

t_ch: 先前我还看见小鸟飞到它肩上休息呢。嘻…不如把它也打扮成「开拓」的样子吧。
t_en: I even spotted birds perching on its shoulder. Hehe... Maybe we should dress it up like the Trailblazer.

t_ch: （上前看看…）
t_en: (Step forward to look)

t_ch: 呀，要小心哦……
t_en: Ah, be careful...

t_ch: 这片麦田可是我们的宝贝，不能随便踩进去哦。会把希望踩坏的。
t_en: This wheat field is our baby! You can't just stomp on it willy-nilly. You'll stomp all over our hopes.

t_ch: 还是交给我吧。下次见面时，{NICKNAME}的小稻草人就布置妥当啦。
t_en: Leave this to me. When we meet again, the {NICKNAME} scarecrow will be firmly in place.

t_ch: 不过，还真是美丽的麦浪呀。等到收割的日子，它就会平息，变成一片金色的湖面……
t_en: But these ripples of wheat are gorgeous. On harvest day, they will finally subside and turn into a golden lake.

t_ch: 到时候，就让{NICKNAME}用麦穗给人家编个头环吧♪
t_en: When the time comes, {NICKNAME} can make wreaths of wheat for everyone ♪

t_ch: 田边的土埂将你绊了一跤，你差点就要与麦田亲密接触了——不过有人拉住了你的手。
t_en: You trip over a ridge by the field and nearly take a dive into the wheat, but someone grabs your hand just in time.

t_ch: 昔涟如约更换了稻草人，这个家伙正盯着你——似乎对鸟儿更可亲了。
t_en: As promised, Cyrene has replaced the scarecrow. The new scarecrow stares at you, seemingly even more approachable to birds.

t_ch: 远眺美景的人们都很贪心，总觉得视野还不够宽阔。
t_en: Those who gaze upon beautiful sights are immensely greedy. They are plagued by a feeling that their horizons aren't wide enough.

t_ch: 我也是个贪心的人，所以要坐得更高一些。这样收进眼底的风景也会更多。
t_en: I'm a greedy person too. So, I want to sit a little higher. That way, I can take in more of the scenery before me.

t_ch: 说来奇怪，在海边的时候明明离得更近，却总觉得遥远无比。
t_en: It's strange. We were closer at the beach, but it felt so far beyond reach.

t_ch: 而坐在屋顶的时候，反倒感觉远方的景色触手可及呢。
t_en: But when I'm sitting on the rooftop, I feel that such distant sights are within my grasp.

t_ch: 距离产生美。
t_en: Distance brings beauty.

t_ch: 是这样吗？可是我和{NICKNAME}坐得很近呀。
t_en: Is that so? But we are seated so closely.

t_ch: 如果能坐得更高就好了。
t_en: If only we could be even higher up.

t_ch: 毕竟哀丽秘榭只是个小村庄，去往其他城邦，我们一定能找到更高的观景台吧？
t_en: Aedes Elysiae is just a small village. If we visit a different city, we can definitely find a higher observation deck, right?

t_ch: 旅途前方还会有更多美景。
t_en: There are even more captivating sights awaiting on this journey.

t_ch: 是呀。启程前行，远方的风景就真的触手可及啦。
t_en: Yes. Once the journey begins, those distant lands will be truly within reach.

t_ch: 看哪，白色的鸟儿们，正向着我们感慨的美景飞翔呢。
t_en: Look, those white birds are soaring toward the mesmerizing scenery.

t_ch: 天空那么高，你说，我们俩会不会也是它们眼中的风景呢？
t_en: The sky is so high. Do you think we're part of a scenery they gaze upon too?

t_ch: 昔涟不再言语，转头静静地望着远方。
t_en: Cyrene falls silent, turning her head to look into the distance.

t_ch: 于是，你也沉浸在视线那端的风光。远山…帆船…海平线……
t_en: So, you also admire the scenery before you. Mountains faraway... Sailboats... The horizon over the sea...

t_ch: 海风让你的脸颊微微发痒，不知是朝霞还是夕照的柔光使你的心情愈发平静。
t_en: The ocean breeze caresses your cheeks. A gentle light, possibly the break of dawn or a sunset glow, calms your heart.

t_ch: 哦，你喜欢这里的风景呀？让我也好好看看，记下你喜欢的地方~
t_en: Oh, you like the view here? Then let me get a good look too, so I can remember the places you like~

t_ch: 坐了好久，都被屋顶的瓦片硌疼啦。下次在这里放两个枕头吧。
t_en: The roof tiles hurt after sitting for too long. Let's bring two cushions over next time.

t_ch: 对了，我不在的时候，可不许自己一个人跑到屋顶上哦？别让人家担心。作为替代，人家还会带你来屋顶看风景的！
t_en: Oh, when I'm not around, you can't come up to the roof alone. I'll be worried. In exchange, I'll bring you up on this roof to look at the view.

t_ch: 我们，在屋顶待了多久啦？
t_en: How long have we been sitting up here on the rooftop?

t_ch: 哀丽秘榭的风景，还真是怎么都看不够呢…下去的时候要小心哦，慢慢地，可别滑倒啦。
t_en: No matter how long you look, this view of Aedes Elysiae never gets old... Be careful when you're climbing down. Take it slow, and don't slip!

t_ch: 真好呀，是那种软软的垫子。{NICKNAME}，来躺一会儿吧？
t_en: Wow, it's a soft cushion. {NICKNAME}, want to lie down for a bit?

t_ch: （躺下来）
t_en: (Lie down)

t_ch: （站在原地）
t_en: (Stay put)

t_ch: 怎么，难道是…害羞了？来尝试一下嘛。
t_en: What's the matter? Are you... shy? Come on over and give it a try.

t_ch: 就算是熟悉的人，一起平躺下来，侧着脸看向对方，也会感觉十分不同哦？
t_en: Even if it's a close friend, lying down and looking at each other from the side will feel very different.

t_ch: 看，天空一片晴朗。望着白云飘过，岁月的脚步仿佛也变慢了。
t_en: Look, the sky is so clear. Watching the white clouds wafting by seems to slow the passage of time.

t_ch: 书上说，每当夜晚降临，会有一颗贼星划过永夜之帷的画布。它会偷听人们许下的愿望，悄悄将其实现……
t_en: I read that when night falls, a Thief Star will streak across the Veil of Evernight. It will eavesdrop on the wishes people make and quietly fulfill them...

t_ch: 不过，人家连贼星的尾巴都没见过。真想知道，比天空更遥远的地方会有什么呀。
t_en: But I've never even seen the shadow of the Thief Star. I really wonder what's beyond the sky.

t_ch: 身为开拓者，你曾目睹「黑塔」空间站的端沿起火燃烧，也见识过梦想在阿斯德纳的黑暗中闪耀……
t_en: As the Trailblazer, you've witnessed the fires blazing at Herta Space Station and dreams shining in the darkness of Asdana...

t_ch: 如果把这些故事分享出来，她一定会很感兴趣吧。
t_en: If you share these stories with her, I'm sure she'll be pleased.

t_ch: 讲讲空间站「黑塔」的见闻。
t_en: Talk about Herta Space Station.

t_ch: 讲讲贝洛伯格的见闻。
t_en: Talk about Belobog.

t_ch: 讲讲仙舟「罗浮」的见闻。
t_en: Talk about the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 讲讲匹诺康尼的见闻。
t_en: Talk about Penacony.

t_ch: 不知道有没有人和{NICKNAME}说过…你讲故事的神态仔细又专注，甚至比故事本身更吸引人呢。
t_en: Has anyone told you this, {NICKNAME}? You're so meticulous and focused when you tell stories, you become more captivating than the stories themselves.

t_ch: 真有意思呢，你这么一说，人家都想去实地看看啦。只是，要你来当向导！
t_en: That's so fascinating! When you talk about it like that, I wanna go see it for myself! Hehe, but I'll want you to be the guide!

t_ch: 谢谢你，为人家解答了疑惑——
t_en: Thanks for clearing that up for me—

t_ch: 原来，在比天空更遥远的地方，时时刻刻发生着那么多浪漫的事呀……
t_en: Turns out there are so many beautiful, romantic things happening beyond even the farthest reaches of the sky...

t_ch: 昔涟睡得很香。也许是哀丽秘榭的麦浪适合入梦，或是簌簌的林叶有助眠的魔法……
t_en: Cyrene is fast asleep. Perhaps the golden wheat fields of Aedes Elysiae is perfect to induce sleep, or maybe the rustling of leaves have a certain magical sleeping aid effect...

t_ch: 「其实是因为在记忆中留下了你的照片，所以感到特别安心哦？」——你仿佛听见昔涟这么说了。
t_en: "Actually, it's because your photo was left in the memory, there's a certain sense of safety to it." ...It felt as if you've heard Cyrene say that.

t_ch: 但她静谧的睡靥明明没有变化…除了嘴角不知何时勾起的笑意。
t_en: And yet, her tranquil sleeping face hadn't changed at all... except for the faint smile that had somehow curled at the corner of her lips.

t_ch: 没钓上鱼，但给口还挺沉的…来看看钓了个什么吧。
t_en: Didn't catch a fish, but the tug was pretty strong... Let's see what it did reel in.

t_ch: 呼…玩捉迷藏还挺累人的呢。你的胜负欲可真强，屋顶上、麦堆里，什么地方你都敢钻呀。
t_en: Phew... Hide-and-seek can be quite tiring. You're really competitive. You'll dive into any spot, whether it's on the roof or in a wheat pile.

t_ch: 可惜都被我找到了！哀丽秘榭还是站在人家这一边的嘛。
t_en: Too bad for you, I found you every time! Aedes Elysiae is on my side, after all.

t_ch: 可惜这里没有垃圾桶……
t_en: Too bad there are no trashcans here...

t_ch: 欸，你要垃圾桶做什么？
t_en: Huh? What do you want trashcans for?

t_ch: …感觉是不能细想的事情呢。
t_en: ...Doesn't sound like something I should get into.

t_ch: 是我输了。
t_en: I lost.

t_ch: 呀，好坦诚……
t_en: Whoa, that's frank...

t_ch: 那么，作为输家的惩罚就是…要是有朝一日人家消失了，你也要相信我会回到你的身边，不可以轻言放弃哦？
t_en: As a loser, your punishment is... If someday I were to disappear, you have to believe that I'll return to your side, don't give up on that thought easily!

t_ch: 在树庭被水淹没的无措？
t_en: The helplessness of drowning in the Grove?

t_ch: 被我喊变美少女的窘迫？
t_en: Embarrassed by me calling you a pretty girl?

t_ch: 到底是什么，你快说呀！
t_en: What is it, just say it!

t_ch: 迷迷糊糊地，居然睡过去了……
t_en: I was so groggy, I actually fell asleep...

t_ch: 昔涟对着小稻草人捣鼓了半天，来看看她的劳动成果吧。
t_en: Cyrene fiddled with the little scarecrow for a long time, let's take a look at her handiwork.

t_ch: 怀揣对天外世界的憧憬，昔涟眼中星光闪烁，向你挥手暂别。
t_en: With her eyes twinkling with the stars of her longing for the world beyond, Cyrene waves goodbye to you.

t_ch: 而在启程前，如果{NICKNAME}有心情，<color=#dbc291ff>随时可以来找我</color>。人家会在这里一直等着你的♪
t_en: In case {NICKNAME} feels up to it before we set off, you <color=#dbc291ff>can come find me anytime</color>. I'll be waiting for you here~ ♪

t_ch: 与你形似神更似的稻草人正冲着你笑——这是昔涟送你的见面礼。记得她说的话吗？要是有心情，去找她玩玩吧。
t_en: The scarecrow, resembling you in both form and spirit, is smiling at you — this is Cyrene's welcome gift. Do you remember what she said? If you're in the mood, go play with her.

t_ch: 记得你和人家抱怨过有本名叫「版本」的书，也总会在最勾人心弦的地方中断。或许这就是命运的常态吧？
t_en: You complained to me before about a book called Versions that always stops at the most captivating parts. Perhaps that's just how fate works, huh?

t_ch: 真奇怪，会掉哪儿了呢…呀，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: Strange, where did I drop it... Oh, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 想做些有意义的事。
t_en: I want to do something meaningful.

t_ch: 想四处看看，多了解这里。
t_en: We could hang around here and get to know this place better.

t_ch: 暂时不想做什么。
t_en: I don't feel like doing anything right now.

t_ch: 诶，{NICKNAME}都学会抢答啦，我还没问你呢…看来，你已经完全融入哀丽秘榭的生活了呢。
t_en: I've yet to speak, but I see you've learned to take the initiative... You're all settled into Aedes Elysiae now, aren't you?

t_ch: 只是…抱歉，这次可能不太行哦。我好像丢了一件很重要的东西。
t_en: It's just... Sorry, this is a bad time. I think I've lost something very important.

t_ch: 对不起，说出来有些为难，我也不知道丢失的是什么，只是被莫名的遗失感萦绕着，觉得自己应该要去寻找……
t_en: Sorry, I can't really explain it, but I don't know what I've lost. Yet, I feel an inexplicable sense of loss, and I feel like I should be searching...

t_ch: 唔…越说越奇怪了，要不，我们还是边走边想吧？
t_en: Hm... The more I think about it, the odder it gets. How about we ponder this matter over a walk?

t_ch: 水面有动静了！快快，把鱼竿提起来，拜托啦！
t_en: Look, ripples! C'mon, reel it in! Please!

t_ch: 想听故事吗？我有好多好多，可以一个个讲给你听。条件只有一个——要仔细听，好吗？
t_en: A story? I have so many I can tell you, but you'll have to listen to each and every one carefully. Alright?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}总是那么耐心地陪伴人家呢，一起慢慢的收集着美好的瞬间……
t_en: You're always so patient with me, slowly collecting all these beautiful moments together...

t_ch: 我钓鱼的时候也很耐心。
t_en: I'm also very patient when fishing.

t_ch: 我听故事的时候也很耐心。
t_en: I'm also very patient when listening to stories.

t_ch: 只要陪着你，我就会很耐心。
t_en: I'm willing to be patient as long as I'm with you.

t_ch: 嗯。不知不觉，已经发生这么多事了呀……
t_en: Yeah. Before we know it, so much has happened...

t_ch: 我想到自己丢失的是什么了。跟我来吧，{NICKNAME}，我们一起去找到它。
t_en: I recall what I've lost. Follow me, {NICKNAME}. Let's search for it together.

t_ch: 真有意思呢，你这么一说，人家都想去实地看看啦。只是，要你来当向导！
t_en: That's so fascinating! When you talk about it like that, I wanna go see it for myself! Hehe, but I'll want you to be the guide!

t_ch: 哦，你喜欢这里的风景呀？让我也好好看看，记下你喜欢的地方~
t_en: Oh, you like the view here? Then let me get a good look too, so I can remember the places you like~

t_ch: 这里呀…不得不说，人家好好地苦恼了一阵子呢——我到底应不应该对它感到熟悉？
t_en: Oh, this place... I must admit, I've mulled over it for quite some time now. Is this somewhere I should be familiar with?

t_ch: 不过，现在也算想通啦，你的到来，为它平添了不一样的回忆，让我真正喜欢上了这里呢。
t_en: But I've thought things through. Your arrival has filled it with unique memories, and I've truly fallen in love with this place.

t_ch: 我记得给你讲过天外故事。
t_en: I remember telling you stories from beyond the sky.

t_ch: 我记得屋顶的瓦片硌屁股。
t_en: I remember how the roof tiles were painful to sit on.

t_ch: 只记得挺充实的。
t_en: I can only recall feeling content.

t_ch: 曾经以为准确无误的记忆，却因为你的陪伴，在故地重游时呈现出了不同的面貌……
t_en: Those memories I once thought were accurate have taken on a new appearance in the stories because you're by my side.

t_ch: 这么说，我们旅途中经历的一切，一定也能亲手让那些沉重的过去，焕发出新的意义吧？
t_en: In a sense, everything we experienced in our journey together will personally give the solemn past new meaning, right?

t_ch: 这片麦田可是我们的宝贝，不能随便踩进去哦。会把希望踩坏的。
t_en: This wheat field is our baby! You can't just stomp on it willy-nilly. You'll stomp all over our hopes.

t_ch: 呼~感觉有点困了…是时候闭上眼睛，去梦里相见啦……
t_en: *yawn*~ I'm feeling kinda sleepy... Time for some shuteye. See you in dreamland...

t_ch: 回到这里，似乎还能听见当时的欢声笑语呢。{NICKNAME}还记得这里发生过什么吗？
t_en: Returning here, I can still hear our laughter from back then. {NICKNAME}, do you remember what happened here?

t_ch: 一起看过麦浪。
t_en: We watched the ripples of wheat together.

t_ch: 拍照留影。
t_en: We took a photo.

t_ch: 记得挺开心的。
t_en: We had a great time.

t_ch: 悄悄告诉你一个秘密，哀丽秘榭的麦田，会永远地留在丰收的时刻。
t_en: I'll let you in on a secret. The wheat fields of Aedes Elysiae remain eternally in the season of harvest.

t_ch: 也许经过启程、冒险，最终回到这里时，我们已经不一样了。但麦浪依旧会是记忆中的样子，一如既往欢迎我们。
t_en: Perhaps we're no longer the same as we were before we departed on a journey, experienced adventures, and returned here. However, these ripples of wheat will welcome us just as how we remember them to be.

t_ch: 果然，在这里呀……
t_en: I knew it'd be here...

t_ch: 丢的是缎带……
t_en: I lost a ribbon...

t_ch: 可是你身上的缎带还在啊。
t_en: But you still have your ribbon on you.

t_ch: 女孩子的缎带怎么可能只有一个嘛。不过，有些事人家也说不清楚。
t_en: A girl always has more than one ribbon. But some things are hard to explain.

t_ch: 一些我们以为丢失的，其实一直都在。一些我们突然找回的，却又总是不愿相信。
t_en: The things we once thought were lost might have been with us all along, while other things we suddenly find may be unbelievable.

t_ch: 哎呀，会不会太多愁善感了？别担心，毕竟已经找到了嘛，我可没有失落哦？
t_en: Oh dear, am I being too sentimental? Don't worry, I've found it now, haven't I? I don't feel down.

t_ch: 虽然我很想说…与你共度的这段时光，都会像这哀丽秘榭一样，<color=#dbc291ff>永远留在无人叨扰的记忆之境。</color>
t_en: Though I have to say... The times we spent together will be like Aedes Elysiae, <color=#dbc291ff>preserved in a tranquil memory space forever.</color>

t_ch: 不过，「永远」这个词…对人家来说，反倒不太浪漫。
t_en: But the word "forever"... isn't something romantic to me.

t_ch: 在记忆黯淡的时候，我们依然能够跨越所有距离，把目光投回「当时」，让自己与那些重要的瞬间——再度{RUBY_B#初见}重逢{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: Even when our memories fade, we can still traverse all distances and look back at the "past" to {RUBY_B#encounter}reunite{RUBY_E#} with those treasured moments.

t_ch: 等到我们拾回珍重之物，将它们装进行囊、决定翻开故事崭新一页的时候……
t_en: By the time we retrieve what we cherish, pack it into our bags, and decide to turn to a new page in our story...

t_ch: 故乡是很让人怀念，可更重要的，是迈出向前的脚步，对吧？
t_en: People always miss their home, but what's more important is to take the next step forward, right?

t_ch: 昔涟从海边找到的一枚缎带，与她身上所佩者模样相同，只是看上去有些磨旧，好似缝埋着岁月的低语。
t_en: A ribbon Cyrene found by the sea — just like the one she wears now, though this one's rather worn out. It feels like it's been sewn together by the whispers of time itself.

t_ch: 真好~有你陪着，简单的日常也变得迷人了。
t_en: This is nice~ Just having you here make even the ordinary feel enchanting.

t_ch: 哀丽秘榭的闲暇时光还很漫长…{NICKNAME}要经常来找人家玩哦！
t_en: There's still plenty of time for leisure in Aedes Elysiae... {NICKNAME}, you should come visit me often!

t_ch: 看上去你和人家一样，都很期待彼此下一次美好的同行吧？
t_en: You look just as excited as I am for our next wonderful journey together, don't you?

t_ch: 不要急嘛~稍微离开一阵子，也许只是睁眼、闭眼的重逢，就有全新的邂逅等待着我们呐~
t_en: Don't rush! Maybe if we take a little break, a reunion that feels like just the blink of an eye could lead to a whole new encounter waiting for us.

t_ch: 你看，三千万世…是不是感觉离「永远」已经很近了？人家可是确确实实失去了这么久的昔涟的记忆哦。
t_en: You see, thirty million lifetimes... Feels pretty close to forever, right? That's how much of Cyrene's memories I've lost.

t_ch: 在那「永远」之中，一定有着应当永志不忘的故事，可现在怎么追忆都找不到了。
t_en: In that "eternity," there must be stories that should never be forgotten, but now no matter how I try to recall them, I can't find them.

t_ch: 但幸运的是，作为迷迷，与你初见的这一世所积攒的记忆，能够成为了一点星光——照亮三千万世那灰黯的遗忘。
t_en: But fortunately, as Mem, the memories accumulated from this first encounter with you can become a guiding starlight — illuminating the dull oblivion of thirty million lifetimes.

t_ch: 而我们一起在哀丽秘榭度过的这段美好时光，让人家愈发觉得，与其奢望「永远」，不如相信……
t_en: And the wonderful time we spent together in Aedes Elysiae makes me feel even more that, rather than wishing for "eternity," it's better to believe...

t_ch: 你好呀…♪
t_en: Hello... ♪

t_ch: {NICKNAME}惊讶的样子，也和记忆里如出一辙呢。
t_en: {NICKNAME}'s surprised expression is just like what the memories recorded.

t_ch: 你认识我…？
t_en: You know me...?

t_ch: 与其说「认识」，不如说是「记得」呢。
t_en: Rather than "knowing" you, it might be better described as... I "remember" you.

t_ch: 你还活着…？
t_en: You're still alive...?

t_ch: 嘻，好新奇的搭讪技巧呀。
t_en: Hehe, what an interesting new way to chat someone up.

t_ch: 你是昔涟，还是迷迷？
t_en: Are you Cyrene or Mem?

t_ch: 我有许多问题想问你…
t_en: I have so many questions for you...

t_ch: 我知道，{NICKNAME}一定有许多疑惑，对吗？虽然很想为你解答，但我也对现状感到手足无措……
t_en: Yes, you must be really perplexed, {NICKNAME}. I wish I could give you answers, but I feel helpless about the current situation too...

t_ch: 是我呀，{NICKNAME}。是人家变化太大，你认不出来了？
t_en: It's me, {NICKNAME}. Don't you recognize me? Or did I really change that much?

t_ch: 是迷迷啊…
t_en: You're Mem...

t_ch: 没能取回完整的记忆吗？
t_en: So, it isn't possible to restore your memories completely?

t_ch: 人家也觉得不可思议。就像做了一场长长的梦，昔涟的记忆、迷迷的记忆，交织在一起…醒来后，只剩下零星片段。
t_en: It's pretty unbelievable for me too. It's like I had a long, long dream. Cyrene's memories... and Mem's memories... they're all tangled together. But when I woke up... there were only broken fragments left.

t_ch: 我已经记不清那三千万个轮回…幸好，我还记得这一世的那些黄金裔伙伴，以及{NICKNAME}你……
t_en: I can no longer remember those thirty million cycles... Luckily, I still remember my Chrysos Heir companions from this life, and you, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 大家都为这个世界牺牲了一切，我也心系着他们的命运，想要尽快开启新的篇章。只是……
t_en: Everyone sacrificed everything for this world, and my heart is tied to their fates too. I want to start a new chapter as soon as possible. It's just that...

t_ch: 人家有时也会因此懊丧…不过换个角度想，在面临重要的抉择之前，静下心来整顿思路也是很重要的。正好，哀丽秘榭最适合做这事啦。
t_en: Sometimes I get frustrated because of that... but looking at it another way, it's really important to calm down and clear your head before making a big decision. And Aedes Elysiae is the perfect place for that.

t_ch: 卸下沉重的心情，深呼吸——然后，作为再次邂逅的纪念，请允许我为{NICKNAME}送上一份礼物吧？
t_en: Cast off your heavy feelings, take a deep breath — and as a memento of our reunion, please allow me to offer you a gift, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 三、二、一……
t_en: Three, two, one...

t_ch: 瞧！这个稻草人的模样，是不是和你特别像？我可是观察了好久才做出来的。
t_en: Check it out! Doesn't this scarecrow look just like you? I spent ages observing just to get it right!

t_ch: 这样一来，哀丽秘榭也种下了「开拓」的种子呢。把它当作下一段旅途的起点……
t_en: Now, we've also planted the seeds of the Trailblaze in Aedes Elysiae. Take it as the start of another journey...

t_ch: 等到时机成熟，我们就再回到这里，一起翻开故事的下一页吧？
t_en: And when the time is ripe, let us return here and write the next page of our story together.

t_ch: 我记得刚见人家时，你心里有许多疑问需要解答。
t_en: I remember you had many questions for me when we first met.

t_ch: 那就让我们约好…在新的旅程开始的时候，要重新认识彼此，一起翻开故事的下一页哦！
t_en: Let's make a promise... When a new journey begins, we will learn about each other again and write the next page of our story together!

t_ch: 你知道吗？在哀丽秘榭的古语中，人们会将「初见」，念作「寻获」，又或者……「重逢」。
t_en: Do you know that in the ancient language of Aedes Elysiae, "first encounter" is read as "searching" or... "reunion."

t_ch: 真好~有你陪着，简单的日常也变得迷人了。记得再来找我玩哦♪
t_en: This is nice~ Just having you here make even the ordinary feel enchanting. Come hang out with me again sometime, okay? ♪

t_ch: 毫无疑问，你曾在故事里见过这个庞大的木制道具，瞧它那高高昂起的头颅，以及宽广到足以装进好几个人的空洞腹部——所有特征都与你的认知相符。
t_en: There is no doubt about it. The giant wooden prop before you is one you recognize from an old story. Its head raised high in the sky and room in its stomach for several people — it has all the familiar characteristics you expect.

t_ch: 你朝周围环视了圈，确定没有人看着这边。嘿，既然有这个机会，为什么不来验证下自己的猜想呢？
t_en: You survey the surrounding area to make sure no one is watching. Well, why not use this opportunity to test your theory?

t_ch: （钻进木马的肚子里）
t_en: (Climb inside the wooden horse's stomach)

t_ch: 这不是无名客该做的事！
t_en: This is not something a Nameless should be doing!

t_ch: 对，恪守职责当然是好习惯，但仔细想想，探索未知的区域怎么就不能算是一种「开拓」呢？
t_en: That's right. It is only proper to maintain one's professional responsibilities. However, if you think about it, isn't exploring the unknown a form of "Trailblazing" in itself?

t_ch: （钻进木马的肚子里）
t_en: (Climb inside the wooden horse's stomach)

t_ch: 还是算了吧。
t_en: Never mind.

t_ch: 好吧，你的理智最终获得了胜利。没关系，看起来这只木制的小马并不会自己跑掉，你还有很多时间来思考究竟该如何行动。
t_en: Alright, your rationality wins out in the end. It's fine. It doesn't look like this wooden horse is going to go running off on its own. You have some time to consider the best course of action.

t_ch: …胜利纪念碑都建好了吗？还有这些，异邦人留下来的战利品，现在是我们的了！
t_en: ...Has construction on the victory monuments been finished? All of this loot that the travelers left behind is all ours now!

t_ch: 来几个人，把东西搬走！兵器收进库里，剩下的送给长官！
t_en: Bring some men and have them take it away! Take the weapons to the storehouse and take the rest to the Captain!

t_ch: 咯噔——你感受到了明显的震动，有人正在搬动你藏身其中的这匹木马。不知道他们此行的目的地是哪，这个时候你最好……
t_en: Clunk! You sense an obvious tremor. Someone has come to transport the wooden horse that you are now hiding inside. You have no idea where they could be taking you, so right now it would be best to...

t_ch: （冲出去）
t_en: (Charge out)

t_ch: 安全高于一切。你尝试推开木马腹部隐藏的活门，却发现…门板纹丝不动。
t_en: Safety first. You try to push open the hidden door in the horse's stomach, only to find... It's shut fast.

t_ch: ——你被关在木马里面了！
t_en: — You're trapped inside the wooden horse!

t_ch: （想办法让外面的人注意到）
t_en: (Think of a way to get their attention)

t_ch: <size=28>喂——等等，我在里面，你们要带我去哪？</size>
t_en: <size=28>Hey! Wait! I'm inside here. Where are you taking me?</size>

t_ch: …是不是最近在竞技场里呆太久了，我怎么好像听到有人在求救？
t_en: ...I feel like I've been spending too much time in the arena lately. I feel like I can hear someone... calling for help?

t_ch: 那是隐秘的召唤，我的兄弟，你正在与伟大的神性直接沟通，让我们把脚步加快些，将纷争的贡品呈送到尼卡多利面前吧。
t_en: It is a secret calling, my brother. You are communicating directly with a great divinity. Let us speed up our pace and present the spoils of strife before Nikador.

t_ch: ——你的呼喊没有起到任何作用，反而让他们把搬运的速度加快了！
t_en: — Your calls were completely ineffective. In fact, it just got them moving you even faster!

t_ch: （继续保持安静）
t_en: (Keep quiet)

t_ch: 在陌生的环境里，保持冷静才是上策。你决定什么都不做，静观事情的后续发展。
t_en: When in an unfamiliar environment, it is always best to keep calm. You decide to take no action and see how things unfold.

t_ch: 不知道在黑暗中颠簸了多久，正当你感觉自己快要吐出来的时候，木马突然被重重地丢在了地上，随之而来的是整齐的列队声。
t_en: You have no idea how long you've been bumping around in the dark. But, just as you feel like you can't hold the contents of your stomach back any longer, the wooden horse comes suddenly crashing to the ground, followed by the sound of soldiers in neat formation.

t_ch: …先放在这里。走，跟我去搬其他东西。
t_en: ...Leave it there for now. Let's go. There's more where that came from.

t_ch: 确认所有声音都消失后，你使尽全身力气，终于砸开了木制的活门，来到了外面——
t_en: After confirming that all sound outside has disappeared, you use all your strength to burst through the wooden trap door and return to the outside world.

t_ch: 然后…迎面撞上了一位悬锋卫兵打扮的壮硕男性。
t_en: Then... You find yourself face-to-face with a powerful-looking man in Kremnos guard uniform.

t_ch: 你是哪个城邦的士兵，来这儿做什么？！
t_en: What city do you fight for, soldier!? Why are you here!?

t_ch: 交代清楚！若有半分踟蹰，尼卡多利的长矛将贯穿你的胸膛！
t_en: Explain yourself! If I sense a hint of hesitation, the long spear of Nikador will pierce your chest!

t_ch: 我也是悬锋城的人！
t_en: I am from Castrum Kremnos!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，赞美塔兰顿！你的教诲为我指明方向，你的公正令付出终有回报。
t_en: Hahaha, praise Talanton! Your teachings guide my path, and your fair orders always yield reward.

t_ch: ——疯王的追随者，找的就是你！没想到吧，我不是悬锋城的人！
t_en: — The Mad King's followers, you're the one I seek! You had no idea, did you? I am not from Castrum Kremnos at all!

t_ch: 怎么还有反转的？
t_en: Another twist?

t_ch: 耍的好啊！
t_en: Well played!

t_ch: 我在城中潜伏了数日，就为刺探疯王的情报，顺便送几个倒霉蛋去见塞纳托斯。可没想到这里的道路竟也如疯王的头脑般令人狂躁，至今仍未有收获。
t_en: I have been undercover in the city for a number of days. I came to seek out information on the Mad King and send a few unlucky fools to meet Thanatos along the way. Though, I had no idea that the routes around here would be as maddening as the Mad King's mind itself. I've made no progress this whole time.

t_ch: 好在，你自己送到了我面前。我将以你的血，作为盛在托盘里的首个砝码！
t_en: Fortunately, you delivered yourself to me on a platter. I shall use your blood as the first weight placed onto the scales!

t_ch: 等等，我刚才是乱说的！
t_en: Wait! I was lying!

t_ch: 辩解只会令你的处境更为艰难。不过，赞美塔兰顿，我有了个新主意。
t_en: Trying to excuse yourself will only make your situation worse. Although, praise Talanton, I just had a new idea.

t_ch: 兵卒的性命于这场战争而言无关痛痒，但若是手握实权的将领，定能令天秤倾斜。
t_en: The life of a mere soldier is expendable in a battle like this. It is those of the power-wielding generals that can really tip the scales.

t_ch: 别冲动，我可以帮你带路！
t_en: Please stay calm. I can be your guide!

t_ch: 明智的决断。兵卒的性命于这场战争而言无关痛痒，但若是手握实权的将领，定能令天秤倾斜。
t_en: A wise decision. The life of a mere soldier is expendable in a battle like this. It is those of the power-wielding generals that can really tip the scales.

t_ch: 告诉我——悬锋城里地位最高的将领，是什么模样？
t_en: Tell me. What is the highest-ranking general of Castrum Kremnos like?

t_ch: （描述悬锋报名官的外观）
t_en: (Describe the appearance of the Kremnos registration officer)

t_ch: 她是…红色头发，穿红色衣服的年轻女性…说话的态度很凶……
t_en: She... She is a young woman. She has red hair and wears red clothing... She speaks with a ferocious attitude...

t_ch: （描述掌粮官的外观）
t_en: (Describe the appearance of the food provisions officer)

t_ch: 他是…灰色头发和胡子的男性…挺老的，不像是会打仗的人……
t_en: He... He is a man with gray hair and a gray beard... He's quite old, and he doesn't look like someone fit for battle...

t_ch: （描述悬锋教官的外观）
t_en: (Describe the appearance of the Kremnos drillmaster)

t_ch: 他是…头发和胡子都全黑的男性，中等年纪，穿红色的衣服……
t_en: He... He is a man with black hair and a black beard. He's middle-aged, and he wears a red outfit...

t_ch: （描述自己的外观）
t_en: (Describe your own appearance)

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}的头发是灰色的，穿着长外套，看起来就特别厉害，从未输给过任何人，手里还经常提着一根球棒……
t_en: {F#She}{M#He} has gray hair, wears a long jacket and looks extremely strong. {F#She}{M#He} has never lost to anyone and is often seen with a baseball bat in {F#her}{M#his} hands...

t_ch: 我见过形形色色的信徒，但会如此美化自己的，你还是第一个。
t_en: I have met all sorts of disciples in my time, but never have I met one that embellishes their identity like you.

t_ch: 是因为战争的气息太过浓烈，以至于士兵的神智都模糊不清，误以为自己便是那指挥全军的将领了么？无论如何，看来我选错了人质。
t_en: Could it be that the spirit of battle has overwhelmed your sanity, causing you to become confused? Does a mere soldier believe themself to be a general of the whole army? Whatever the case, it seems that I have picked the wrong person as a hostage.

t_ch: 罢了，你走吧，我没时间再去听那些胡话…祝你早日康复。
t_en: Forget it. Just go. I have no time to indulge your nonsense... May you recover your sanity soon.

t_ch: 不错。与我收集的信息一致，你的形容确实与记录里的某位军官相符合。快走吧，塔兰顿绝不徇私，你为赢得你的自由放置了足够的砝码。
t_en: Not bad. Your description matches that of an officer I have collected information on. Be gone. Talanton bears no personal grudges. You have given enough weight to win your freedom.

t_ch: 而我，将以此身投入天秤——
t_en: As for me, I shall give my body to tip the balance...

t_ch: 等等，我是奥赫玛的人！
t_en: Wait! I am from Okhema!

t_ch: …刻法勒的信者？看你的衣着整齐，不像是上战场的人，倒确有几分可能。
t_en: ...A disciple of Kephale? Judging by your proper appearance, I guess I can believe that you were not one for the battlefield.

t_ch: 罢了，你是哪个泰坦的追随者都无所谓，塔兰顿衡量万物的价值——你出现在此处，或许能为我提供新的出路。
t_en: Though it matters not which Titan you follow. Talanton judges the value of all things. You appearing before me may present me with a new path.

t_ch: 原来你不是尼卡多利的随从？
t_en: So, you're not a follower of Nikador?

t_ch: …你怎么穿着悬锋城的盔甲？
t_en: ...Why are you wearing the armor of Castrum Kremnos?

t_ch: 必要的伪装罢了。我与你相同，都是趁乱混进城内，打算刺探疯王的情报，顺便送几个倒霉蛋去见塞纳托斯。
t_en: It is no more than a necessary disguise. Much like you, I took advantage of the chaos of battle to make my way into the city. I came to seek out information on the Mad King and send a few unlucky fools to meet Thanatos along the way.

t_ch: 话虽如此，没想到这里的道路竟也如疯王的头脑般令人狂躁，军众狂热，戒备森严，难以四处走动，至今未能有收获。
t_en: That being said, I had no idea that the paths in this city were as maddening as the Mad King's mind itself. The soldiers and civilians are frantic, and there are guards all around. It's been hard to move around, so I have made no progress at all.

t_ch: 若你真是那全知全能{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的信者，那就告诉我——我该前往何处寻找仇敌，好结束这野蛮的战事？
t_en: If you are truly a disciple of the all-knowing, all-powerful {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, then tell me... Where should I go to find my enemy and bring this brutal conflict to an end?

t_ch: （指向奥赫玛的方向）
t_en: (Point in the direction of Okhema)

t_ch: 谎言。那是出城的路，莫非你希望我放下手中兵器，投奔奥赫玛？
t_en: Lies. That is the path out of the city. Perhaps you are hoping that I would lay down my weapons and seek asylum in Okhema?

t_ch: 但我肩负部族最后的期望，若是临阵脱逃，日后有何面目祭奠他们，又该以什么名义继续苟活于世？
t_en: But I bear the final hope of my people. If I were to desert, what right would I have to uphold their memory? What name would I live under in this world?

t_ch: 你当真认为我该就此离开？
t_en: Do you really think that I should just leave like this?

t_ch: 确定，你没法结束这场战争。
t_en: I am certain that there is nothing that you can do to end this war.

t_ch: 我会考虑你的提议。战争本就毫无荣誉可言，但我有必须做的事…无论如何，愿塔兰顿庇佑你平安返回。
t_en: I will consider your suggestion. Though there is no honor in war, there is something I must do... Whatever happens, may Talanton protect you and ensure your safe return.

t_ch: 我是说着玩的。
t_en: I'm just kidding.

t_ch: （询问他的仇敌是谁）
t_en: (Ask who his enemy is)

t_ch: 兵卒的性命于这场战争而言无关痛痒，但若是手握实权的将领，定能令天秤倾斜。我要找的，就是悬锋城中地位最高的将领。
t_en: The life of a mere soldier is expendable in a battle like this. It is those of the power-wielding generals that can really tip the scales. I seek the highest-ranking general in Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 你能说出他的模样么？
t_en: Can you describe him to me?

t_ch: （描述悬锋报名官的外观）
t_en: (Describe the appearance of the Kremnos registration officer)

t_ch: 她是…红色头发，穿红色衣服的年轻女性…说话的态度很凶……
t_en: She... She is a young woman. She has red hair and wears red clothing... She speaks with a ferocious attitude...

t_ch: （描述掌粮官的外观）
t_en: (Describe the appearance of the food provisions officer)

t_ch: 他是…灰色头发和胡子的男性…挺老的，不像是会打仗的人……
t_en: He... He is a man with gray hair and a gray beard... He's quite old, and he doesn't look like someone fit for battle...

t_ch: （描述悬锋教官的外观）
t_en: (Describe the appearance of the Kremnos drillmaster)

t_ch: 他是…头发和胡子都全黑的男性，中等年纪，穿红色的衣服……
t_en: He... He is a man with black hair and a black beard. He's middle-aged, and he wears a red outfit...

t_ch: （描述自己的外观）
t_en: (Describe your own appearance)

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}的头发是灰色的，穿着长外套，看起来就特别厉害，从未输给过任何人，手里还经常提着一根球棒……
t_en: {F#She}{M#He} has gray hair, wears a long jacket and looks extremely strong. {F#She}{M#He} has never lost to anyone and is often seen with a baseball bat in {F#her}{M#his} hands...

t_ch: 我见过形形色色的信徒，但会如此美化自己的，你还是第一个。
t_en: I have met all sorts of disciples in my time, but never have I met one that embellishes their identity like you.

t_ch: 是因为战争的气息太过浓烈，以至于士兵的神智都模糊不清，误以为自己便是那指挥全军的将领了么？无论如何，看来我选错了人质。
t_en: Could it be that the spirit of battle has overwhelmed your sanity, causing you to become confused? Does a mere soldier believe themself to be a general of the whole army? Whatever the case, it seems that I have picked the wrong person as a hostage.

t_ch: 罢了，你走吧，我没时间再去听那些胡话…祝你早日康复。
t_en: Forget it. Just go. I have no time to indulge your nonsense... May you recover your sanity soon.

t_ch: 嗯…不错。与我收集的信息一致，你的形容确实与记录里的某位军官相符合。快走吧，愿塔兰顿庇佑你平安返回。
t_en: Not bad. Your description matches that of an officer I have collected information on. Go quickly. May Talanton protect you and ensure your safe return.

t_ch: 而我，将以此身投入天秤——
t_en: As for me, I shall give my body to tip the balance...

t_ch: 你曾藏身其中的木马，现在已经失去了意义。或许你可以尝试去追踪那位独身潜入悬锋城的战士，毕竟…你为他指明了道路。
t_en: The wooden horse that you were hidden inside has now lost all meaning. Perhaps you can try and track down the lone warrior that had infiltrated Castrum Kremnos. You did give him directions, after all...

t_ch: 覆满了血污的盔甲，看得出它的主人曾经历过漫长而残酷的厮杀。但你认出了刻在头盔中央的小小的天秤标志，你记得这位忠于信念的战士。
t_en: A suit of armor that is covered in blood stains. It is clear to see that its owner experienced a long and brutal struggle. However, you recognize the insignia in the center of the helmet of a set of scales and recall the soldier that remained true to their faith.

t_ch: 献给尼卡多利的祭典仍在继续，疯狂的泰坦与城邦还远远没有走到尽头。
t_en: The sacrifices to Nikador continue. The mad Titan and the insane city are far from done.

t_ch: ——愿塔兰顿给予他与牺牲相等价的馈赠。愿他的灵魂安息。
t_en: ...May Talanton reward him with a gift fitting of his sacrifice. May his soul rest in peace.

t_ch: 你注意到盔甲上有被火舌灼烧过的痕迹，以及刻在头盔中央的小小的天秤标志。这位忠于信念的战士，或许他曾尝试去引发一场火灾，但烈焰最终只吞噬了他自己。
t_en: You notice that the armor shows signs of being burnt, as well as an insignia of a set of scales carved into the center of the helmet. The soldier who remained true to his faith may have attempted to start a fire here, only to end up being engulfed in the flames himself.

t_ch: ——愿塔兰顿给予他与牺牲相等价的馈赠。愿他的灵魂安息。
t_en: ...May Talanton reward him with a gift fitting of his sacrifice. May his soul rest in peace.

t_ch: 残破的盔甲被随意丢弃在一旁，表面遍布着刀剑砍伐的痕迹，但你认出了刻在头盔中央的小小的天秤标志，你记得这位忠于信念的战士。
t_en: The damaged suit of armor has been carelessly cast aside, covered in sword slashes. However, you recognize the insignia in the center of the helmet of a set of scales and recall the soldier who remained true to his faith.

t_ch: 你不知道他究竟遭遇了什么，但结局已然清晰摆在你的面前，或是一队偶然路过的卫兵，或是冰冷无情的眷属…无论如何，悬锋城容不下除胜利之外的任何事物。
t_en: You have no idea what it was that happened to him, but the ending he met is clear to see. Perhaps he happened to bump into a troupe of wandering guards or a merciless Titankin... Whatever it may have been, Castrum Kremnos will tolerate no outcome but victory.

t_ch: ——愿塔兰顿给予他与牺牲相等价的馈赠。愿他的灵魂安息。
t_en: ...May Talanton reward him with a gift fitting of his sacrifice. May his soul rest in peace.

t_ch: 全新的盔甲被整齐放在道路旁的箱子里，看来它的主人已不再需要在悬锋城中活动了。但你认出了刻在头盔中央的小小的天秤标志，你记得这位忠于信念的战士。
t_en: A brand-new set of armor has been carefully stored in a box by the path. It seems that its owner no longer had any business in Castrum Kremnos. However, you recognize the insignia in the center of the helmet of a set of scales and recall the soldier who remained true to their faith.

t_ch: 你相信，那座「永恒之城」会接纳所有愿意在刻法勒光芒下安居的人们，哪怕他来自不同信仰的城邦，哪怕他的手中曾沾满鲜血。
t_en: You firmly believe that the Eternal City will take in anyone willing to live under the light of Kephale, even those from cities of other faiths... Even those with blood on their hands.

t_ch: ——愿他能平安抵达。你在心中如此祈愿。
t_en: ...In your heart, you pray that he arrives safely.

t_ch: 还没等你调整好躺卧的姿势，外面忽地传来了说话声。
t_en: Before you even have the chance to make yourself comfortable, you suddenly hear a voice from outside.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，你曾在故事里见过这个庞大的木制道具，瞧它那高高昂起的头颅，以及宽广到足以装进好几个人的空洞腹部——所有特征都与你的认知相符。
t_en: There is no doubt about it. The giant wooden prop before you is one you recognize from an old story. Its head raised high in the sky and room in its stomach for several people — it has all the familiar characteristics you expect.

t_ch: 你不明白它为何会出现在这里。能勉强与这匹木马扯上关系的唯有{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}，可命运三相殿早已荒废，那位孩童模样的半神同样不在此处。
t_en: You cannot understand why this would appear here. This wooden horse could only feasibly be connected to the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}, but the Temple of the Three Fates has long since fallen into ruin, and that child-like demigod is nowhere to be seen.

t_ch: 你左右张望，都没能见到任何人影。既然如此，恐怕只能靠你来一探究竟了——
t_en: You look around you, but there is no one in sight. It seems that it is up to you to investigate...

t_ch: （钻进木马的肚子里）
t_en: (Climb inside the wooden horse's stomach)

t_ch: 这不是无名客该做的事！
t_en: This is not something a Nameless should be doing!

t_ch: …是吗？没想到你如此地忠于「开拓」职责，可想想那些被你翻过的垃圾桶、反复研究的路灯，还有不知道砸了多少个的盾牌…真的不打算钻进去看看吗？
t_en: ...Really? I had no idea you were so faithful to your duty as a Trailblazer. But just think about all of those trash cans you've flipped over, all of those street lamps you've investigated over and over, the countless shields you've smashed... Are you really not going to go inside and take a look?

t_ch: （钻进木马的肚子里）
t_en: (Climb inside the wooden horse's stomach)

t_ch: 还是算了吧。
t_en: Never mind.

t_ch: 要是{RUBY_B#大地之泰坦}吉奥里亚{RUBY_E#}在场，你的勤勉与节制绝对能获得它的嘉奖。可惜它并未回应，你还有足够的时间来思考究竟该如何行动。
t_en: If {RUBY_B#Earth Titan}Georios{RUBY_E#} were here, your diligence and self-control would have no doubt been commended. Unfortunately, there was no response. Although, you still have plenty of time to consider what action to take.

t_ch: 木马内部比预想得还要宽敞，带着些微的温暖，你可以很轻松地舒展开身体。正当你还在研究如何躺着会更舒服时，外面忽地传来了说话声——
t_en: The inside of the wooden horse is even more spacious than you imagined. There is a faint warmth to it and you can stretch out comfortably. Just as you are investigating the most comfortable position to lie down in, a voice suddenly comes from outside —

t_ch: 伟大的万径之门，雅努斯啊，我知道你的神性尚未泯灭，还游荡于往世的断壁残垣中。
t_en: Great Gate of Infinity! Janus! I know that your divinity has not been eradicated, that you still roam the ruins of our past world!

t_ch: 若你能听到信徒的祈祷，请给予我回应吧，请…轻敲三次木壁，以昭示你的存在。
t_en: If you can hear your disciple's prayer, please give me a sign. Please... knock on this wood three times and show that you are here.

t_ch: （轻敲三次木马的内侧）
t_en: (Knock on the inside of the wooden horse three times)

t_ch: （什么都不做）
t_en: (Do nothing)

t_ch: 你只是想找个安静地方躺着而已，至于回应信徒的祈祷这种麻烦事，还是留给那些愿为神圣事业献身的高尚人士吧。你翻了个身，打算换个更舒服的姿势。
t_en: You just wanted to find a quiet place to lie down. When it comes to answering the prayers of disciples, leave that to the noble folks who are willing to devote themselves to their holy calling. You turn over, trying to find a more comfortable position.

t_ch: …这时，你的球棒从口袋中掉了出来，不偏不倚正好砸在木马的壳上。
t_en: ...At that very moment, your baseball bat falls from your pocket and happens to strike the frame of the wooden horse.

t_ch: 咚、咚、咚——清脆的敲击声在神殿中回荡，其音空灵悠长，像是从遥远的时空传来。
t_en: Knock, knock, knock... The crisp knocking sound echoes through the temple. The sound is ethereal and drawn-out, as if it had originated from a distant space-time.

t_ch: 雅努斯在上，您竟然真的回应了！赞美您的仁慈与怜悯！
t_en: Janus above! You have responded to me! Praise your kindness and mercy!

t_ch: 我请求您的神谕，我有困惑想得到您的启示。请别再对现世的苦难保持沉默，若是您拒绝给出解答，我将长久守在此处，直到命运之线被剪断，直到神殿崩塌！
t_en: I request your divine foresight. I am lost in confusion and wish for your revelation. Please do not remain silent in the face of the struggles of the world. If you refuse to enlighten me, I shall remain right here until the threads of destiny are cut and the temple collapses around me!

t_ch: …好吧，看来这位信徒的执着远超你的想象。怎么办，你打算如何回答呢？
t_en: ...Right, looks like this disciple is far more persistent than you could have imagined. How will you respond?

t_ch: （让对方表示对你的虔敬）
t_en: (Make them show their admiration)

t_ch: 你使劲敲打了几下木壁，试图向那位信徒传递这样的信息——你很生气，希望暴躁的情绪得到安抚，最好的办法就是对你献上供品。
t_en: You hit the wooden wall hard a few times, trying to convey this message to your disciple — You are very angry, and your raging emotions must be appeased. The best way to do this would be with an offering.

t_ch: 啊，我知道了！您是在为我的不敬而愤怒对吧，既见神迹，当然要跪拜。我这就把您喜爱的物品放在神迹前，供您享用。嗯，都放好了。
t_en: Ah, I understand! My disrespect has enraged you, hasn't it? When in the presence of a miracle, it is only right for me to bow before you. I will arrange your favorite items before this miracle for you to enjoy. There, they are all in place.

t_ch: （先向对方表示你的善意）
t_en: (Express your benevolence)

t_ch: 你轻轻敲打了几下木壁，试图向那位信徒传递这样的信息——你是个温和而宽容的神，关爱世人，愿意聆听信者的任何请求。
t_en: You gently tap the wooden wall a few times, trying to convey this message to your disciple — You are a gentle and forgiving god. You care for the people of this world and are willing to listen to any request your believers may have.

t_ch: 啊，我知道，您从未抛弃过我们！既见神迹，当然要跪拜。这些是您喜爱的物品，我悉数呈放在神迹前，供您享用，以示虔敬。您看，都放好了。
t_en: Ah, I knew it! You never abandoned us! When in the presence of a miracle, it is only right for me to bow before you. These are your favorite items. I present them before this miracle for you to enjoy as a sign of my reverence. See, they are all here for you.

t_ch: 神意既显，我也向您坦白我的困惑——我即将因公务前往神悟树庭，在离开奥赫玛前，我想知道…走出城门时，我该先迈左脚还是右脚？
t_en: Now that your godly will is revealed, I too shall confess my concern. Soon, I am to go to Grove of Epiphany on official business. Before I leave Okhema, I need to know... Should I step out of the city with my left or right foot?

t_ch: 仔细想想，这还是你第一次被当作神明对待。降下的首个神谕，竟然是这种颇为无厘头的问题？你不禁怒从心生，所以打算——
t_en: Thinking about it, this is the first time that you have been approached as a god. This is your first ever divine prophecy, and it is for such an inane question? You cannot help but feel rage growing inside you, so you...

t_ch: （狂敲左边的木壁）
t_en: (Slam wildly on the left side wall)

t_ch: 左左左…还是左！我明白了，您是希望我先迈左脚是吧。感谢您的教诲！
t_en: Left, left, left... And another left! I understand. You wish for me to step out on my left foot, correct? Thank you for your guidance!

t_ch: （狂敲右边的木壁）
t_en: (Slam wildly on the right side wall)

t_ch: 右…右右右，还是右…您的意思是，我该先迈右脚？虽然不太习惯，但我尽量。
t_en: Right... Right, right, right, and another right... You mean that I should step out on my right foot? That seems unnatural to me, but I will try my best.

t_ch: （胡乱敲打木壁）
t_en: (Randomly slam against the wooden wall)

t_ch: 左左…右右…上上…下下…您突然发怒，是在说我不该前往神悟树庭吗？我明白了。
t_en: Left, left... Right, right... Up, up... Down, down... This sudden rage of yours... does it mean that you do not wish for me to go to Grove of Epiphany? I understand.

t_ch: （什么都不做）
t_en: (Do nothing)

t_ch: 您不回答…是困了？…您还在吗？喂…喂……
t_en: You aren't responding... Are you tired? ...Are you still there? Hello... Hello...?

t_ch: 伟大的万径之门，雅努斯都对这个问题保持沉默，或许…我就不该前往树庭。
t_en: Even the great Gate of Infinity, the mighty Janus, falls silent before this quandary. Perhaps... I should not go to the Grove of the Muses?

t_ch: 我的疑惑得到了解答，就不打扰了。供品在神迹前，请您慢用。
t_en: My concerns have been answered. I will disturb you no longer. Your offerings are before your miraculous shrine, and I hope that you will enjoy them.

t_ch: 等了许久，外面都不再有说话声传来。你确信那位虔敬的信徒已经走了，正当你缓慢推开木马的活门，打算呼吸新鲜空气的时候……
t_en: You wait a considerable while until you hear no further sound from the outside. Confident that the loyal disciple had gone, you gently push the trap door open and go to take a breath of fresh air, when...

t_ch: 啊——还有件事忘了向您讲…不对，你是谁啊？
t_en: Ah, there's one more thing I wanted to speak with you about... Wait a minute, who are you?

t_ch: 我是雅努斯的信徒。
t_en: I am a disciple of Janus.

t_ch: 哈？别硬扯关系，我参加聚会这么多次，从来就没见过你。算了，有外人在场，想必雅努斯是不会亲临了，我明天再来。
t_en: Huh? Don't try and get chummy with me. I've been to so many disciple meetings and not once have I seen you there. Forget it. There is no way Janus would appear before me again if there is an outsider here. I will come back tomorrow.

t_ch: 我就是雅努斯本人！
t_en: I am the great Janus!

t_ch: …秘酿喝多了，晕头了？我看你是法吉娜的信徒才对吧！倒霉，我明天再来！
t_en: ...Your brain is pickled on brew, isn't it? You must be a disciple of Phagousa! Curses. I'll come back tomorrow!

t_ch: 你曾藏身其中的木马，前方摆放着那位虔诚信徒献上的供品，看起来似乎很诱人。
t_en: Before the wooden horse that you were hidden inside are a number of offerings from your pious disciple. They look most tempting.

t_ch: 拿走供品。
t_en: Take the offerings.
